title: Tapping for Generosity Without Fear
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing the worry that giving reduces your financial safety
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 54 of a 12-week money program. The script works with the fear that generosity depletes: the holding back when money felt tight, the worry that giving now means less later. The reframe moves toward generosity as part of how wealth circulates.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 54 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when the urge to give (a tip, a gift, a donation) is followed by a flash of 'but what if I need that.'

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 54 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the fear-of-depletion response that can follow a generous impulse: the quick calculation, the holding back, the guilt afterward. The reframe builds generosity as something that belongs in a wealthy life, not something you earn the right to once the account reaches a particular number.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though giving used to feel scary when money felt tight, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I feared generosity would mean less for me, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I've sometimes held back from giving, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I've worried that giving reduces my safety.
Side of eye: I've hesitated to share when income was low.
Under eye: I feared I wouldn't have enough left.
Under nose: I've questioned if I can afford generosity.
Chin: I sometimes held on too tightly.
Collarbone: I'm ready for a different relationship with giving.
Under arm: I let generosity feel natural and safe.
Top of head: I welcome giving as part of wealth.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I love sharing from abundance.
Side of eye: Giving multiplies wealth and joy.
Under eye: I trust money flows back in many forms.
Under nose: I enjoy blessing others with what I have.
Chin: I build generosity into my finances.
Collarbone: I feel richer when I give.
Under arm: I create a cycle of giving and receiving.
Top of head: I make generosity a joyful part of wealth.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 54 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
